channel.title: Marketing on Nils Myszkowski, PhD
channel.description: Recent content in Marketing on Nils Myszkowski, PhD

item.title: How Subjective Processing Fluency Predicts Attitudes Toward Advertisements and Purchase Intention
item.description: What it’s about In this paper, we examine how subjective processing fluency predicts attitudes toward advertisements and purchase intention. Abstract This paper examines the focal construct and reports empirical and/or psychometric evidence relevant to its interpretation. The findings contribute to the literature by clarifying measurement, theoretical implications, and practical applications in context. How to access the paper You can access the full paper here.
